target.title: feat: include provider/model/profile/trigger context in overloaded and rate limit error messages
target.body:
## Problem

When an LLM provider returns any kind of error — rate limit, overloaded, or a network/transport failure — the user sees a terse message with zero actionable context:

> The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment.
> ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later.
> LLM request failed: network connection error.

No indication of which provider/model was involved, which auth profile, what triggered the request, why it actually failed, or what to do about it.

## Solution

All three error classes now include context and an actionable hint:

**Overloaded:**
> ⚠️ The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment. [anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, profile=manual, trigger=heartbeat, req=req_01Xyz] Tip: add a fallback model to avoid waiting (see model fallback config).

**Rate limit:**
> ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later. [google/gemini-2.0-flash, trigger=heartbeat, session=agent:main:main] Tip: add a fallback model or switch to a higher-quota API key.

**Network errors** — each variant now explains the specific cause and fix:
> LLM request failed: the provider API refused the connection. [anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, trigger=cron] This usually means the provider endpoint is down or a local proxy/firewall is blocking it. Check your internet connection and provider status page, or add a fallback model.

> LLM request failed: the connection to the provider was dropped mid-request. [...] Often a transient network hiccup — retry usually works. If it persists, check your network stability or add a fallback model.

> LLM request failed: DNS lookup for the provider endpoint failed. [...] The provider hostname could not be resolved — check your internet/DNS, or verify the API base URL in your config.

> LLM request failed: network connection error (connect ECONNREFUSED ...). [...] Could not reach the provider — check your internet connection or provider status. Adding a fallback model can help ride out transient outages.

Context fields (only included when available):
- **provider/model** — e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`
- **profile** — the auth profile ID that was active
- **trigger** — what initiated the run: `user`, `heartbeat`, or `cron`
- **session** — session key for tracing
- **req** — Anthropic `request_id` from the raw JSON payload (useful for support)

All context values are sanitized (control characters stripped) so they can't corrupt single-line log output.

## Changes

- `errors.ts`: add `extractRequestId()`, `extractRawErrorReason()`, `sanitizeContextValue()`
- `errors.ts`: add `TransientErrorOpts` type and `buildTransientErrorContext(raw, opts)` helper (takes raw for request_id extraction, sanitizes all values)
- `errors.ts`: update `formatRateLimitOrOverloadedErrorCopy()` with context + hint
- `errors.ts`: update `formatTransportErrorCopy()` — each branch now has a specific cause description, workaround hint, and full context
- `errors.ts`: extend `formatAssistantErrorText()` opts with `profileId` and `trigger`; thread through to both formatters
- `run.ts`: pass `lastProfileId` and `params.trigger` at both `formatAssistantErrorText` call sites
- `run/payloads.ts`: add `profileId`/`trigger` to `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` params
- Tests: update exact-string assertions to `toContain`/`stringContaining` for enriched messages

## Related issues

Closes #43932 — Rate limit errors surfaced to chat should include model/provider/task context
Closes #51336 — Feature Request: Surface API provider name in error/overload messages
Closes #30262 — Surface actionable error context on LLM provider timeouts instead of generic messages

target.title: Hide command-output titles when progress uses status mode
target.body:
## Summary

- omit `command-output` titles when channel progress is configured with `commandText: "status"`
- keep existing raw/default behavior unchanged so command-output titles still render outside status mode
- add a regression assertion for failed command-output progress lines

## Why

`commandText: "status"` already suppresses command text for command/tool progress, but `command-output` lines still appended `input.title`. That can expose internal command titles in channel progress even when the channel is configured for status-only command display.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A `command-output` progress event with `commandText: "status"` should render the status/exit code without appending the command-output title.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, running the real `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts` formatter through `node --import tsx`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Create a `command-output` event with `exitCode: 1` and a title.
2. Render it with default options.
3. Render the same event with `{ commandText: "status" }`.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node --import tsx - <<'EOF'
import { formatChannelProgressDraftLine } from './src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts';
const input = {
  event: 'command-output',
  name: 'exec',
  exitCode: 1,
  title: 'run internal config check failed',
};
console.log('default:', formatChannelProgressDraftLine(input));
console.log('status:', formatChannelProgressDraftLine(input, { commandText: 'status' }));
EOF
default: 🛠️ exit 1; run internal config check failed
status: 🛠️ exit 1
```

Observed result after fix: Default/raw behavior still includes the title. Status mode renders only `exit 1`, so the command-output title is suppressed.

What was not tested: Full Discord end-to-end rendering was not run locally; the changed formatter is the shared channel progress path used by channel previews.

## Test

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`

target.title: fix: register google-gemini-cli in external CLI credential sync
target.body:
## Summary

The `google-gemini-cli` provider was missing from the `EXTERNAL_CLI_SYNC_PROVIDERS` list in `external-cli-sync.ts`. This meant that OpenClaw could not auto-discover and reuse Gemini CLI OAuth credentials from `~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, forcing users through a full OAuth PKCE flow (manual verification code copy-paste) every time they opened a new terminal session.

All other CLI providers (Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI/Codex, MiniMax) already had this credential sync wired up. This PR adds the same treatment for Gemini CLI.

### Changes

- **`src/agents/auth-profiles/constants.ts`**: Added `GEMINI_CLI_PROFILE_ID` constant (`"google-gemini-cli:default"`)
- **`src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.ts`**: Registered `google-gemini-cli` in `EXTERNAL_CLI_SYNC_PROVIDERS` using the existing `readGeminiCliCredentialsCached` function from `cli-credentials.ts`

### Why this works

The `readGeminiCliCredentials` function (and its cached variant) already existed in `cli-credentials.ts` — it reads `~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, parses the `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `expiry_date`, and decodes the `id_token` JWT for identity (email, sub). The only missing piece was wiring it into the external CLI sync loop so that the auth profile store would automatically pick up these credentials at startup.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo --noEmit`: ✅ zero type errors
- `pnpm build`: ✅ success
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles/external-cli-sync.test.ts`: ✅ 38/38 pass
- `pnpm test src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts`: ✅ 75/75 pass
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles/external-auth.test.ts`: ✅ 16/16 pass
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles/store.test.ts`: ✅ 94/94 pass
- Local manual verification: confirmed `~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` exists and is correctly parsed

target.title: fix #78919: [Bug] ACP sessions_spawn doesn't route images to Codex's native vision like acpx codex exec does
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #78919

### Issue
[[Bug] ACP sessions_spawn doesn't route images to Codex's native vision like acpx codex exec does](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78919)

### Changes
- fix(agents): route image attachments through sessions_spawn ACP path to Codex native vision

### Changed Files
```
CHANGELOG.md                                 |  1 +
 src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts                 | 72 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 src/agents/acp-spawn.ts                      | 24 ++++++++++
 src/agents/agent-command.ts                  |  3 ++
 src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts | 25 +++++++---
 src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.ts      |  9 +---
 6 files changed, 119 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: When calling `sessions_spawn(runtime=acp)` with image attachments, the tool rejected them with "attachments are currently unsupported for runtime=acp". Meanwhile, `acpx codex exec` correctly forwarded images to Codex native vision. The images were silently dropped instead of being routed through the ACP gateway agent call.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw dev checkout on Linux (CentOS 8), Node 22, with `pnpm tsc` type checking and unit tests via `pnpm test`.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-78919
pnpm install
npx tsc --noEmit
pnpm test src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts
pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:
Terminal output from tsc and test runs confirms the fix compiles and tests pass:
```
$ npx tsc --noEmit
(no errors)

$ pnpm test src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts
 ✓ acp-spawn > toRpcAttachments > converts utf8 attachment to base64
 ✓ acp-spawn > toRpcAttachments > preserves base64 attachment
 ✓ acp-spawn > toRpcAttachments > forwards mimeType when present

$ pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts
 ✓ sessions_spawn tool > ACP runtime > forwards attachments to spawnAcpDirect instead of rejecting
```
The code change removes the early-return error block that rejected attachments for `runtime=acp`, and instead forwards them through `spawnAcpDirect` → `acpManager.runTurn` via the new `toRpcAttachments` helper that encodes images to base64 for the RPC call.

**Observed result after fix**: `sessions_spawn(runtime=acp)` no longer rejects image attachments. Images are forwarded through the ACP path via `toRpcAttachments` and `acpManager.runTurn`, matching the working `acpx codex exec` image routing path. TypeScript compilation and all related unit tests pass cleanly.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end live gateway test with actual Codex vision model (tested via unit test coverage only).

target.title: fix(#85334): prevent bundled plugin load paths from being injected into config
target.body:
## Background

During `openclaw update 5.19 → 5.20`, the Post-Core Convergence step (`post-core-plugin-convergence.ts`) calls `repairMissingConfiguredPluginInstalls` after the in-update doctor has already returned. This calls `addPluginLoadPath` for bundled plugins (e.g., `memory-wiki`), writing their paths to `plugins.load.paths` without checking if they resolve to OpenClaw's own bundled plugin directory. Bundled plugins are auto-discovered and do not need a config entry.

Standalone `doctor --fix` does NOT inject the path — it only fails to clean it up because `maybeRepairBundledPluginLoadPaths` runs mid-sequence and later repair steps re-add it.

The result: every `openclaw update` adds a redundant bundled path to `openclaw.json`, and subsequent `doctor --fix` runs warn about it.

## Changes

### A — `src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.ts`

Added a bundled root guard in `addPluginLoadPath()`. Before writing any plugin path to `plugins.load.paths`, check whether it resolves to the bundled plugin directory via `resolvePackagedBundledLoadPathAlias` — the same function used by `discovery.ts` to emit the redundant-path warning. Bundled paths are skipped entirely.

This prevents the update's post-core convergence step from injecting the path.

### B — `src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.ts`

Moved `maybeRepairBundledPluginLoadPaths` to the **end** of the repair sequence. Standalone `doctor --fix` can now properly clean up paths that were injected by a previous update.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Prevent `openclaw update` and `doctor --fix` from injecting redundant bundled plugin paths into `plugins.load.paths`, and ensure existing stale paths are properly cleaned up.

**Real environment tested**:
- Mac mini (Apple M2, macOS Sequoia) — npm global install v2026.5.20
- Oracle VPS (4 vCPU Ampere, Ubuntu 24.04) — npm global install v2026.5.20

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Patched compiled JS files on production npm install (onboarding-plugin-install-BePyG74f.js, repair-sequencing-cx_inahG.js)
2. Removed existing stale path from openclaw.json
3. Ran `openclaw doctor --fix`
4. Examined openclaw.json for bundled memory-wiki path
5. Ran `openclaw doctor --fix` again (idempotency check)

**Evidence after fix**:
Before — openclaw.json contained:
```
"load": { "paths": ["/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/memory-wiki"] }
```
After (Fix A + B applied) — openclaw.json is clean:
```
"load": { "paths": [] }
```
The warning `plugins: plugin: ignored plugins.load.paths entry that points at OpenClaw's current bundled plugin directory` no longer appears.

**Observed result after fix**:
- `doctor --fix` completes without injecting bundled paths
- Config stays clean across multiple runs (idempotent)
- Same result verified on both Mac mini and VPS

**Not tested**:
- `openclaw update` end-to-end (requires actual version upgrade)

Closes #85334

target.title: fix(gateway): aggregate usage cost across agents
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets: - Problem: `usage.cost` scanned only one default transcript directory when callers did not pass `agentId`, while `sessions.usage` already discovered sessions across configured agents. - Why it matters: Control UI and menu usage views can show empty or partial daily cost data in multi-agent deployments even when session usage exists under other agents. - What changed: resolve the `usage.cost` agent scope from configured gateway agents, merge daily/totals across agents, include the resolved scope in the cache key, and preserve date-only daily labels in the Control UI. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not implement the dedicated `usage.jsonl` storage proposal from #13219 or a separate cost-unit normalization pass; it addresses the confirmed cross-agent aggregation/scope mismatch reported in #41986. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #13219 - Addresses the confirmed aggregation-scope bug from #41986 - Supersedes #42063 by @stablegenius49; this PR carries forward its core `usage.cost` aggregation direction with additional cache-scope, explicit-agent, UI date-label, and partial-failure coverage. - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: `usage.cost` called `loadCostUsageSummary` without an agent scope, which resolves through the single-agent/default transcript directory instead of the configured gateway agent set. - Missing detection / guardrail: gateway tests covered multi-agent discovery for `sessions.usage`, but not the sibling `usage.cost` aggregate path, non-`main` default agent ids, or browser-visible daily usage rendering for date-only buckets. - Contributing context (if known): #13219 describes a broader usage tracking enhancement; this is a narrower current-state aggregate fix. #42063 previously proposed the core aggregation direction, and this PR supersedes it with broader scope validation, cache scoping, UI, and partial-failure coverage. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: `usage.cost` without `agentId` calls the cost summary loader once per configured agent and merges daily/totals; explicit `agentId` remains scoped; configured default agents do not need to be named `main`; date-only daily buckets render with the same calendar day in the Control UI. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the gateway handler boundary, mocked transcript-cost seam, and UI date formatting behavior without needing real transcript files. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for multi-agent `usage.cost`. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Multi-agent deployments should see `usage.cost` totals and daily entries aggregated across configured agents when no `agentId` is requested. Explicit `agentId` requests remain scoped to that agent. The Control UI daily usage chart should no longer show `No data` when cross-agent session usage exists, and date-only daily buckets no longer shift one day backward for users west of UTC. ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text Before: Control UI -> usage.cost(no agentId) -> one default transcript directory only -> Daily Usage: No data After: Control UI -> usage.cost(no agentId) -> configured agents -> merged daily/totals -> Daily Token Usage ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? Yes - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `usage.cost` now reads the same configured agent session scopes that `sessions.usage` already reads. Explicit `agentId` requests are validated and remain scoped, and cache keys include the resolved agent scope. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: host Node `v25.9.0`, pnpm `10.33.0` - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Gateway usage RPC and Control UI - Relevant config (redacted): `agents.list = [{ id: "ops", default: true }, { id: "opus" }]` ### Steps 1. Configure multiple agents with usage-bearing transcripts outside a single default transcript directory. 2. Request `usage.cost` without `agentId`. 3. Compare returned daily/totals with per-agent cost summaries and the Control UI Usage page. ### Expected - The response includes merged daily/totals across configured agents. - The Control UI daily usage chart renders the merged daily bucket instead of `No data`. ### Actual - Before this change, only one default transcript directory was scanned, so the Control UI could show `Daily Usage` / `No data` while `sessions.usage` showed activity. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Trace/log summary: - Before/live gateway `18789`: `usage.cost` for `2026-04-22` returned `daily: []` and zero totals, while `sessions.usage` for the same range returned a populated daily aggregate. - After/PR gateway `18790`: `usage.cost` for `2026-04-22` returned a populated daily bucket with about `117M` tokens and by-type breakdown. - Chrome MCP visual smoke on the PR gateway showed `Daily Token Usage`, `Apr 22`, `April 22, 2026`, and token-type breakdown rows instead of `No data`. - Chrome MCP visual smoke also verified the `Cost` toggle before/after: the official gateway still showed the daily chart as `No data`, while the PR gateway rendered `Daily Cost`, `Apr 22`, `$111.24`, and `COST BY TYPE`. - Screenshots are embedded in this PR description and are not committed to the codebase. Screenshots: Before, current official build showing the scrolled `Daily Usage` section with `No data`: After, PR gateway `18790` showing the scrolled `Daily Token Usage` section populated for `Apr 22`: Before, current official build with `Cost` selected still showing the daily chart as `No data`: After, PR gateway `18790` with `Cost` selected showing `Daily Cost` populated for `Apr 22`: ## Prior Work / Credit This PR supersedes #42063 by @stablegenius49 and carries forward its core direction: make `usage.cost` aggregate across configured gateway agents. The implementation here expands that work with explicit `agentId` validation/scoping, agent-scoped cache keys, partial per-agent failure handling, non-`main` agent coverage, UI date-label handling, and visual verification. Suggested maintainer landing credit, if squashing or direct-landing this PR: `(thanks @stablegenius49)` or `Co-authored-by: stablegenius49 <185121704+stablegenius49@users.noreply.github.com>`. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: multi-agent `usage.cost` aggregate, explicit `agentId` scoping, invalid/out-of-scope explicit `agentId` rejection, cache behavior with agent-scoped cache keys, configured default agent not named `main`, first configured agent fallback, no-agent fallback to `main`, discovered disk agents when no list constrains the gateway, Control UI daily token rendering, and Control UI daily cost rendering against a PR-branch gateway. - Edge cases checked: bounded cache eviction, in-flight cache coalescing, gateway typecheck/lint/import-cycle guards, date-only bucket rendering under `America/Sao_Paulo` timezone. - What you did **not** verify: no known unverified items for this PR scope. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: more transcript directories may be scanned for unscoped `usage.cost` requests. - Mitigation: the gateway already uses the same configured agent list for `sessions.usage`, and `usage.cost` remains cached with the resolved agent scope included in the cache key. ## AI Assistance - AI-assisted: Yes, implemented with OpenAI Codex. - Degree of testing: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, targeted gateway usage/UI Vitest shards (`pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage-metrics.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/usage-helpers.node.test.ts test/official-channel-catalog.test.ts`), `pnpm check:changed`, `pnpm build`, Chrome MCP visual smoke against a PR-branch gateway including `Tokens` and `Cost` modes, and `codex review --base origin/main` at refreshed head `c613510233` with no actionable findings. Current mergeable head `e14c554f32` was refreshed with a normal merge from `origin/main` and revalidated with `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`, the focused usage/UI tests, `pnpm check:changed`, and `pnpm build`. - Session logs/prompts: not attached. - Understanding: I reviewed the agent-scope and gateway usage paths; the production change aggregates the existing transcript-backed cost summaries across resolved gateway agent ids, preserves explicit `agentId` scoping, and fixes date-only daily label rendering. - Local Codex review: `codex review --base origin/main` was run locally and reported no actionable findings.

target.title: fix(agents): use CJK-aware token estimation in compaction path (#70052)
target.body:
## Summary
The compaction path uses `estimateTokens` from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` which assumes ~4 chars/token for all scripts. This severely underestimates CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) content where each character maps to ~1 token, causing compaction to trigger too late and leading to context window overflow.

## Root Cause
The upstream `estimateTokens()` counts `text.length / 4` for all scripts. For CJK text, this yields ~0.25 tokens/char instead of ~1 token/char (a 4x underestimation). OpenClaw already has a CJK-aware estimator (`estimateStringChars` in `src/utils/cjk-chars.ts`) used in the pruning path, but the compaction path was not using it.

clawsweeper's review on this issue confirmed the failing path and explicitly identified this PR as the "active fix PR" when the upstream issue was closed-as-duplicate: *"Source inspection shows current main uses the upstream `chars/4` estimator while the dependency contract confirms CJK text is counted as ~1 token/char"* and *"Close as superseded by the active fix PR #70112. Current main still uses the upstream `estimateTokens` in the reported compaction paths, so this is not implemented-on-main, but #70112 directly tracks and patches the same remaining gap."*

## Changes
- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: Add `cjkTokenCorrection()` helper and `estimateTokensCjkAware()` wrapper that adds CJK correction delta on top of the upstream estimate. Use it in `estimateMessagesTokens()`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts`: Replace all 4 `estimateTokens` references with `estimateTokensCjkAware` (metrics, full session estimation, hook callbacks).
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts`: Replace `estimateTokens` with `estimateTokensCjkAware` for synthetic message estimation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Compaction triggers too late on Korean / Chinese / Japanese conversations because `estimateTokens` (from `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`) divides `text.length` by 4 for ALL scripts, but CJK text is roughly 1 token per character on real-world tokenizers. Result: a Korean conversation that actually consumes ~615 tokens of context is reported as ~195 tokens, so the runtime thinks the window is 3× emptier than reality and lets it overflow before compaction triggers. clawsweeper closed issue #70052 explicitly tagging this PR (#70112) as the active fix.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-70052` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14, executing the patched production exports from `src/agents/compaction.ts` (`estimateTokensCjkAware`, `estimateMessagesTokens`) and `src/utils/cjk-chars.ts` (`estimateStringChars`, `CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE`) directly through `node --import tsx`. No mocks, no test runner — the same production helpers the compaction path calls. PR head: `e5f7bf2d9dd55639d21bcc20b78a10e834f08301`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/cjk-token-estimation && node --import tsx ./smoke-pr70112.ts`, where `smoke-pr70112.ts` imports `estimateTokensCjkAware` / `estimateMessagesTokens` from `./src/agents/compaction.js` and builds three sample messages (Korean, Chinese, English) of comparable lengths to compare the upstream `chars/4` formula against the CJK-aware estimator and a control English message.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `e5f7bf2d9d` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/agents/compaction.ts` and `src/utils/cjk-chars.ts` exercised through their real exports via `node --import tsx`).

~~~text
$ node --import tsx ./smoke-pr70112.ts
=== Character / token reference values ===
CHARS_PER_TOKEN_ESTIMATE = 4

--- korean message ---
  rawLen (UTF-16)            = 780
  codePointLen               = 780
  estimateStringChars        = 2460
  upstream chars/4 estimate  = 195 tokens
  estimateTokensCjkAware     = 615 tokens
  inflation ratio (new/old)  = 3.15x

--- chinese message ---
  rawLen (UTF-16)            = 360
  codePointLen               = 360
  estimateStringChars        = 1395
  upstream chars/4 estimate  = 90 tokens
  estimateTokensCjkAware     = 259 tokens
  inflation ratio (new/old)  = 2.88x

--- english message ---
  rawLen (UTF-16)            = 1700
  codePointLen               = 1700
  estimateStringChars        = 1700
  upstream chars/4 estimate  = 425 tokens
  estimateTokensCjkAware     = 425 tokens
  inflation ratio (new/old)  = 1.00x

=== Conversation total via estimateMessagesTokens ===
  messages = 3
  totalTokens (CJK-aware) = 1299
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: For 780 UTF-16 chars of Korean content the upstream estimate is 195 tokens but the CJK-aware estimator returns **615 tokens** (3.15× inflation, matching the ~1 token/char reality for Korean syllables). For 360 chars of Chinese it returns 259 tokens (2.88× inflation). For 1700 chars of plain English the estimator returns **exactly the same 425 tokens** as before — confirming the correction is additive on top of the upstream estimate and Latin-only conversations are unaffected. `estimateMessagesTokens` over the mixed 3-message conversation reports 1299 tokens, the sum of the per-message CJK-aware estimates, demonstrating the fix reaches the actual compaction-trigger arithmetic.
- **What was not tested**: A full live `runEmbeddedPiAgent` cycle against a real LLM with a CJK transcript hitting the context window was not exercised on the contributor machine (would need an actual provider key and a multi-thousand-message Korean session). The fix is a pure-function character-counting change at the estimator layer; the four compaction call sites (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts`: metrics, full session estimation, hook callbacks) and the preemptive path (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts`) all swap `estimateTokens` → `estimateTokensCjkAware` with identical signatures, so the only runtime change is the (now-accurate) numeric estimate that drives the existing trigger threshold. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live LLM run with a CJK transcript is required.

## Test

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/compaction.test.ts` — 17 passed (existing + new CJK regression cases)
- `pnpm test -- src/utils/cjk-chars.test.ts` — 17 passed
- `pnpm test -- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts` — 9 passed

Closes #70052

target.title: fix: prevent re-entrant loop in internal hook trigger
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes re-entrant infinite loop in `triggerInternalHook` when a handler calls `triggerInternalHook` again with the same `type:action:sessionKey` combination.

## Problem

Before this fix, if a hook handler (e.g., `command:new`) triggered the same hook again internally, it would cause an infinite loop because there was no active-dispatch guard.

## Solution

Added an `AsyncLocalStorage`-based re-entrancy guard:
- Re-entrant calls within the same async call chain are blocked (handler → triggerInternalHook → same key → sees inherited store → dropped)
- Independent concurrent calls from different async contexts are NOT blocked (preserves at-least-once delivery)

## Changes
- `src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`: Added `AsyncLocalStorage` guard around `triggerInternalHook`
- `src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts`: Added 116 lines of tests covering recursive, sequential, and concurrent dispatch behavior

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Re-entrant `triggerInternalHook` infinite loop (same `type:action:sessionKey` triggered within its own handler chain).

**Real environment tested:** Local dev checkout, Node v24.14.0 on linux x64. Standalone reproduction script (`repro-reentrant-guard.mjs`) mirroring the exact guard logic from this PR, run directly with `node` — no monorepo build or test runner required.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-reentrant-guard.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Standalone reproduction script output showing before/after behavior:

```text
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
Real Behavior Proof: AsyncLocalStorage Re-entrant Guard
Date: 2026-05-18T15:53:46.987Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Scenario 1: WITHOUT guard (main branch behavior)
  A handler that re-triggers the same event → infinite loop

Triggering command:new:test-session (capped at 10 to avoid hang)...
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#2) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#3) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#4) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#5) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#6) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#7) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#8) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#9) — command:new:test-session
  [NO GUARD] handler invoked (#10) — command:new:test-session

  Result: handler called 10 times (would be infinite without cap)
  ⚠️  WITHOUT guard, re-entrant calls amplify without bound

Scenario 2: WITH guard (PR branch behavior)
  Same handler → re-entrant call is correctly blocked

Triggering command:new:test-session...
  [WITH GUARD] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:test-session
  [WITH GUARD] re-entrant call BLOCKED ✅ (key: command|new|test-session)

  Result: handler called 1 time(s), re-entrant blocked 1 time(s)
  ✅ WITH guard, re-entrant calls are prevented

Scenario 3: Sequential triggers AFTER first completes → OK

Triggering #1...
  [SEQUENTIAL] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:test-session
Triggering #2...
  [SEQUENTIAL] handler invoked (#2) — command:new:test-session
Triggering #3...
  [SEQUENTIAL] handler invoked (#3) — command:new:test-session

  Result: handler called 3 time(s)
  ✅ Sequential triggers work correctly

Scenario 4: Concurrent INDEPENDENT triggers → both delivered
  (simulates fireAndForgetHook behavior)

Firing 2 independent concurrent triggers for message:received:session-a...
  [CONCURRENT] handler invoked (#2) — message:received:session-a
  [CONCURRENT] handler invoked (#2) — message:received:session-a

  Result: handler called 2 time(s)
  ✅ Independent concurrent triggers are both delivered

Scenario 5: Different session keys → not blocked

Firing command:new for session-a and session-b concurrently...
  [CROSS-SESSION] handler invoked (#1) — command:new:session-a
  [CROSS-SESSION] handler invoked (#2) — command:new:session-b

  Result: handler called 2 time(s)
  ✅ Different session keys are independently dispatched

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SUMMARY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Without guard (main):
    Re-entrant loop → 10 calls (capped, would be infinite)

  With guard (PR branch):
    Re-entrant blocked    → handler called 1x, blocked 1x ✅
    Sequential triggers   → handler called 3x ✅
    Concurrent independent→ handler called 2x ✅
    Cross-session         → handler called 2x ✅

  The AsyncLocalStorage guard prevents re-entrant amplification
  while preserving all legitimate dispatch patterns.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

**Observed result after fix:** Re-entrant calls for the same `type:action:sessionKey` within the same async call chain are correctly blocked (Scenario 2), while independent sequential (Scenario 3), concurrent (Scenario 4), and cross-session (Scenario 5) dispatches all proceed normally. The standalone script confirms the guard behavior matches the production fix.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-process concurrent triggers (single-process only)
- Performance impact under extreme load (normal usage validated)

## Related
- Fixes potential infinite loop when embedded agent turns trigger the same hook type

target.title: Honor OpenAI thinking profiles over stale catalog opt-outs
target.body:
## Summary

- Lets OpenAI/OpenAI-Codex provider-owned thinking profiles override stale catalog `reasoning: false` rows.
- Keeps non-OpenAI `reasoning: false` catalog rows as explicit off-only opt-outs.
- Adds resolver and `/think` directive regressions for `openai/gpt-5.5` and `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

Closes #84646.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`/think xhigh` on canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` could be rejected with `Use one of: off` when stale runtime catalog metadata marked the model `reasoning: false`, even though the OpenAI provider profile advertises non-off thinking levels for that model.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS worktree with bundled Node.js `v24.14.0` and repository dependencies.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs /private/tmp/openclaw-84646-proof/proof.ts
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts
git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:
The standalone runtime proof imports the real thinking resolver and asks for `openai/gpt-5.5` with a stale `reasoning:false` catalog row:
```json
{
  "openaiGpt55LevelsWithStaleFalse": [
    "off",
    "minimal",
    "low",
    "medium",
    "high",
    "xhigh"
  ],
  "openaiGpt55Formatted": "off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh",
  "openaiGpt55XHighSupported": true,
  "googleReasoningFalseLevels": [
    "off"
  ]
}
```

Targeted tests:
```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       123 passed (123)
```

Observed result after fix:
`openai/gpt-5.5` keeps the OpenAI provider thinking profile and accepts `xhigh` even when stale catalog metadata says `reasoning:false`. A separate Google `reasoning:false` catalog row remains off-only, preserving explicit non-OpenAI opt-out behavior.

What was not tested:
I did not run a live Telegram/Pi OAuth session or the full repository test suite. The proof covers the real thinking resolver plus targeted directive/runtime tests for the reported rejection path.

## Attribution

If this PR is squashed or reworked, please preserve the commit author attribution for Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>, or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: [codex] Fix empty result after exec stdout turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes empty user-visible results from Codex exec-only turns where the harness can finish with tool output recorded but no final assistant text.

## Root cause

Two ordering/result-shaping gaps could surface as `payloads=0` even though the command itself succeeded:

- Codex app-server `turn/completed` can arrive while a native tool item is still active, so the projector may finalize before the native `item/completed` is reflected in the snapshot.
- Some exec/bash-only prompts explicitly ask for stdout to be used as the final reply, but the runner previously only promoted trailing tool results for cron `NO_REPLY`.

## Changes

- Defer live `turn/completed` handling until active native tool items complete, while preserving existing buffered-terminal/client-close behavior.
- Add a narrow trailing tool-result promotion path for successful exec/bash stdout when the prompt asks for stdout as the final reply and no assistant/message/client-tool side effects are present.
- Read the real Codex app-server `toolResult` block shape.
- Cover both `final reply` wording and the existing Docker-smoke wording: `Reply with the exact stdout only (trim trailing newline)`.
- Keep existing cron `NO_REPLY` behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Codex exec-only turns could finish with successful native command stdout but no final assistant payload, surfacing upstream as `payloads=0` / `empty_result`. This proof covers the exact stdout-only wording ClawSweeper called out: `Reply with the exact stdout only (trim trailing newline)`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS source checkout of this PR head `d6dd2ada0f3c2e09e3fe178ef4285b857416ec76`, using OpenClaw's local embedded agent command with the real Codex app-server harness, `/opt/homebrew/bin/codex app-server --listen stdio://`, `CODEX_HOME=$HOME/.codex`, and a temp OpenClaw config that explicitly enables `plugins.entries.codex` plus `agents.defaults.models.openai/gpt-5.5.agentRuntime.id = "codex"`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
HEAD=d6dd2ada0f3c2e09e3fe178ef4285b857416ec76
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<temp>/openclaw.json
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state
OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=<temp>/workspace
CODEX_HOME=$HOME/.codex
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug
OPENCLAW_CODEX_DISCOVERY_LIVE=0
OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_BIN=/opt/homebrew/bin/codex
OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_APPROVAL_POLICY=never
OPENCLAW_CODEX_APP_SERVER_SANDBOX=danger-full-access
node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local \
  --session-id stdout-proof-20260522T170908Z \
  --model openai/gpt-5.5 \
  --thinking off \
  --json \
  --timeout 360 \
  --message "Use the exec tool to run this command: cat <temp>/stdout-token.txt. Reply with the exact stdout only (trim trailing newline)."
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted runtime log excerpt from `.artifacts/codex-stdout-proof-20260522T170908Z.log`:

```text
HEAD=d6dd2ada0f3c2e09e3fe178ef4285b857416ec76
EXPECTED_STDOUT=OPENCLAW_STDOUT_PROOF_20260522T170908Z_26472
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
[agent/embedded] codex app-server raw notification received
...
"text": "OPENCLAW_STDOUT_PROOF_20260522T170908Z_26472"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_STDOUT_PROOF_20260522T170908Z_26472"
"finalAssistantRawText": "OPENCLAW_STDOUT_PROOF_20260522T170908Z_26472"
"winnerProvider": "openai-codex"
"fallbackUsed": false
"toolSummary": {
  "calls": 1,
  "tools": ["bash"],
  "failures": 0
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A real Codex app-server exec stdout-only turn produced one visible final payload exactly equal to the command stdout token, with one successful `bash` tool call, `winnerProvider=openai-codex`, and `fallbackUsed=false`.
- **What was not tested:** No known real-behavior gaps for this patch. I did not run the full Docker install E2E; the live proof directly exercises the same exact-stdout-only prompt contract through the real Codex app-server harness from this PR head.

## Validation

```text
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts
=> Test Files  2 passed (2)
=> Tests  216 passed (216)

./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts -t 'defers turn completion until an active native tool item completes'
```

target.title: [codex] Fix chat history for message-tool replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a chat history reload case where a visible source reply sent through the `message` tool could disappear after switching tabs or reloading the desktop UI.

When a run uses `message_tool_only`, the model can send the user-facing text via `message.send` and then finish with `NO_REPLY`. The existing history projection correctly drops the silent `NO_REPLY`, and the UI hides tool calls by default, but that leaves no normal assistant message for reload history.

This change mirrors successful current-session `message.send` tool-call text into a normal assistant display message during `chat.history` projection. Explicitly routed message sends are left as tool events only, so messages sent somewhere else are not copied into the current chat.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: A real desktop/gateway chat history reload could lose a visible assistant reply because the reply text existed only inside a successful `message.send` tool call and the final assistant message was `NO_REPLY`.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw gateway session JSONL from the reported 2026-05-20 01:29 turn in the user's real main-agent session. The public proof below redacts the actual chat text.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the patched `projectChatDisplayMessages` implementation with `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the real gateway session JSONL, then emitted a redacted JSON summary proving the reported turn, successful message-tool result, and projected assistant mirror.

**Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the after-fix local run:

```json
{
  "realSetup": "local OpenClaw gateway session JSONL from the reported 2026-05-20 01:29 turn",
  "sourceMessages": 212,
  "projectedMessages": 203,
  "reportedTurnPresent": true,
  "messageToolResultOk": true,
  "projectedMirror": {
    "present": true,
    "role": "assistant",
    "mirrorMarker": true,
    "textLength": 70,
    "textSha256": "900f14dfdb49689864f1936ec980bac86bb76bd172b8d9666e153d8b9b21f989"
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix**: The patched projection finds the real reported turn, sees the successful `message` tool result, and emits a normal assistant mirror (`projectedMirror.present: true`, `role: assistant`, `mirrorMarker: true`) with the expected redacted reply length/hash. This is the reload path that previously left only the hidden tool call plus dropped `NO_REPLY`.

**What was not tested**: I did not deploy this patch into the installed release gateway or do a live desktop click-through. The after-fix proof is projection-level against the real affected transcript, supplemented by focused gateway tests, lint, and typechecks.

## Validation

Ran locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-webchat-message-tool-reload-history`:

- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`

- keep raw log lines unchanged for export/debugging

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Gateway logs that contain terminal color escape sequences were displayed in the Control UI as raw `\^[[...]` text.

Local macOS checkout with the UI Vitest shard.

`pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/logs.test.ts`

The log parser test now proves ANSI sequences are removed from rendered JSON log fields and from plain log lines, while `raw` remains intact for export.

Observed result after fix:
1 Vitest file passed, 2 tests passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested:
A browser screenshot of the live logs tab; the rendering input is covered at the parser boundary where the raw escape codes enter UI state.

Fixes #64399.

target.title: Honor OpenAI thinking profiles over stale catalog opt-outs
target.body:
## Summary

- Lets OpenAI/OpenAI-Codex provider-owned thinking profiles override stale catalog `reasoning: false` rows.
- Keeps non-OpenAI `reasoning: false` catalog rows as explicit off-only opt-outs.
- Adds resolver and `/think` directive regressions for `openai/gpt-5.5` and `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

Closes #84646.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`/think xhigh` on canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` could be rejected with `Use one of: off` when stale runtime catalog metadata marked the model `reasoning: false`, even though the OpenAI provider profile advertises non-off thinking levels for that model.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS worktree with bundled Node.js `v24.14.0` and repository dependencies.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs /private/tmp/openclaw-84646-proof/proof.ts
PATH=/Users/andy/.cache/codex-runtimes/codex-primary-runtime/dependencies/node/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts
git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix:
The standalone runtime proof imports the real thinking resolver and asks for `openai/gpt-5.5` with a stale `reasoning:false` catalog row:
```json
{
  "openaiGpt55LevelsWithStaleFalse": [
    "off",
    "minimal",
    "low",
    "medium",
    "high",
    "xhigh"
  ],
  "openaiGpt55Formatted": "off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh",
  "openaiGpt55XHighSupported": true,
  "googleReasoningFalseLevels": [
    "off"
  ]
}
```

Targeted tests:
```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       123 passed (123)
```

Observed result after fix:
`openai/gpt-5.5` keeps the OpenAI provider thinking profile and accepts `xhigh` even when stale catalog metadata says `reasoning:false`. A separate Google `reasoning:false` catalog row remains off-only, preserving explicit non-OpenAI opt-out behavior.

What was not tested:
I did not run a live Telegram/Pi OAuth session or the full repository test suite. The proof covers the real thinking resolver plus targeted directive/runtime tests for the reported rejection path.

## Attribution

If this PR is squashed or reworked, please preserve the commit author attribution for Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>, or include:

Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: Keep chat session discovery from hiding older cron sessions
target.body:
Fixes #84537

## Summary

- stop chat/session selector refreshes from inheriting the 120-minute Sessions tab active-window filter
- keep the existing bounded `limit: 100`, agent scoping, global/unknown inclusion, and archive behavior
- update chat/gateway/session-switch tests to lock the unbounded chat discovery request in place

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Older persistent cron/custom chat sessions disappeared from the Control UI selector because chat refresh paths effectively sent `activeMinutes: 120` to `sessions.list`.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS using the bundled Node runtime.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts`

Evidence after fix:
The focused suite passed: 5 test files, 168 tests.

Runtime proof command also exercised `refreshChat()` with `sessionsFilterActive: "120"` and a 3-hour-old cron session. The observed `sessions.list` params were:

```json
{
  "includeGlobal": true,
  "includeUnknown": true,
  "configuredAgentsOnly": true,
  "agentId": "main",
  "limit": 100
}
```

Observed result after fix:
`activeMinutesSentToGateway` was `false`, and the old cron session key `agent:main:cron:daily-planning` remained in `host.sessionsResult`.

What was not tested:
I did not run a live browser upgrade flow against an installed 2026.5.18 bundle.

## Checks

- `git diff --check`

Author attribution note: if this PR is squashed or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include `Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`.

target.title: Policy: add secret and auth conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Policy 1.0 secret provenance and auth-profile conformance checks with redacted config evidence.

Logical base: merged #80783. This PR intentionally stays config-level only: it reads existing OpenClaw config and reports policy conformance findings through `doctor --lint`; it does not add runtime enforcement and it does not take on secrets management.

- Records managed SecretRef/provider provenance without storing secret values.
- Reports unmanaged SecretRefs, denied secret provider sources, insecure secret provider posture, missing auth profile metadata, and unapproved auth profile modes.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and evidence shape.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "secrets": {
    "requireManagedProviders": true,
    "denySources": ["exec"],
    "allowInsecureProviders": false
  },
  "auth": {
    "profiles": {
      "requireMetadata": ["provider", "mode"],
      "allowModes": ["api_key", "token"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Safety

This is policy conformance, not secrets regulation or storage management. `doctor --lint` reads config-level declarations, emits redacted findings, and leaves credential stores and runtime behavior to the existing auth/credential systems.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: config-level Policy conformance for redacted secret provenance and auth-profile metadata/mode rules.

Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-81974-policy` on Ubuntu-24.04; PR head `d72baa8115b12041a1c532479e919480c46eda57` is signed and GitHub-verified.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git push fork policy-81974-config:policy-secrets-auth-provenance`
- `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-81974/openclaw.json timeout 180 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --severity-min error`
- Earlier same-head verification: `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
- Earlier same-head verification: `git diff --check`
- Earlier same-head verification: `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- Earlier same-head verification: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot`
- Earlier same-head verification: `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-81974-original-lint.tsbuildinfo`
- Earlier same-head verification: autoreview local pass #1 and #2 clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Evidence after fix: the real `doctor --lint` CLI run loaded a temp workspace policy and config, ran 37 checks, and returned four redacted policy errors:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 37,
  "checksSkipped": 0,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/auth-profile-unapproved-mode",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/auth/profiles/oauth",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/auth/profiles/allowModes",
      "message": "Auth profile 'oauth' uses mode 'oauth' outside the policy allowlist."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-denied-provider-source",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/secrets/providers/command",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/denySources",
      "message": "Secret provider 'command' uses denied source 'exec'."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-insecure-provider",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/secrets/providers/vault",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/allowInsecureProviders",
      "message": "Secret provider 'vault' enables insecure posture: allowInsecurePath."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-unmanaged-provider",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/models/providers/anthropic/apiKey",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/requireManagedProviders",
      "message": "SecretRef uses unmanaged provider 'missing'."
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: the CLI reported the expected secret/auth conformance findings through `doctor --lint` without printing private secret values.

What was not tested: no live Gateway was launched; this is config-level policy conformance proof only.

## Related

- #80783 - Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
- #81981 - Policy: add gateway exposure checks

target.title: fix(qwen): enable qwen3.6-plus on Coding Plan CN, correct reasoning flag
target.body:
## Summary

- **What changed**: Enables `qwen3.6-plus` on the DashScope Coding Plan CN endpoint (`coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) and corrects its `reasoning` flag (`false` → `true`).
  - `extensions/qwen/models.ts`: `reasoning: false` → `reasoning: true` on the `qwen3.6-plus` catalog entry; `buildQwenModelCatalogForBaseUrl` simplified to return the full catalog for all base URLs (qwen3.6-plus no longer excluded from Coding Plan URLs).
  - `extensions/qwen/openclaw.plugin.json`: removes the two `modelCatalog.suppressions` entries that blocked `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan configurations — confirmed available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`.
  - `extensions/qwen/index.ts`: removes `normalizeConfig` (which stripped `qwen3.6-plus` from Coding Plan `providerConfig.models`); no longer needed after manifest suppression removal.
- **Why it matters**: `qwen3.6-plus` is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` (DashScope CN Coding Plan). The existing suppression incorrectly hid it from Coding Plan users. The `reasoning: false` flag causes OpenClaw to suppress thinking-block rendering for a model that returns `reasoning_content`.
- **What did NOT change**: Auth flow, other model entries, endpoint constants, and Standard endpoint behavior are untouched. Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior is not claimed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations (Qwen / DashScope provider catalog)
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Standalone catalog update; no linked issue.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: `qwen3.6-plus` was suppressed on Coding Plan CN and showed `reasoning: false`. After this patch it is available on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com` and the reasoning flag is accurate.
- **Environment tested**: DashScope CN Coding Plan endpoint (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`), live API key, direct HTTP.
- **Steps run after the patch**:
  1. Sent a completion request for `qwen3.6-plus` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1` with a live Coding Plan API key.
  2. Inspected response for `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirming thinking-mode support.
- **Observed result**: `qwen3.6-plus` accepted on Coding Plan CN endpoint; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` present in response, confirming `reasoning: true` is correct.
- **Evidence**:

<details>
<summary>Live response — qwen3.6-plus on coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com</summary>

```text
POST https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1/chat/completions
Authorization: Bearer sk-<redacted>
```

```json
{
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is 2+2? Answer in one word."}],
  "max_tokens": 50
}
```

```json
{
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "content": "Four",
      "reasoning_content": "Here's a thinking process:\n\n1. Question: \"What is 2+2?\"\n2. 2 + 2 = 4\n3. One word: Four",
      "role": "assistant"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "index": 0
  }],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 22,
    "completion_tokens": 227,
    "total_tokens": 249,
    "completion_tokens_details": {"reasoning_tokens": 221, "text_tokens": 227}
  },
  "model": "qwen3.6-plus"
}
```

`qwen3.6-plus` accepted on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` and `reasoning_tokens` confirmed.

</details>

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- Risk summary: Catalog metadata change only. Removes an incorrect suppression; no new endpoint, auth flow, or runtime surface introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- Endpoint: DashScope CN Coding Plan (`https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`)
- Auth: DashScope Coding Plan API key (`sk-<redacted>`)

### Steps

1. POST completion request with `"model": "qwen3.6-plus"` to `https://coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com/v1`.
2. Inspect response for `reasoning_content` and `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens`.

### Expected

`qwen3.6-plus` responds on Coding Plan CN with `reasoning_content` present.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: `qwen3.6-plus` live on `coding.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`; `reasoning_content` present (flag corrected to `true`).
- **Edge cases checked**: None beyond CN Coding Plan endpoint.
- **What I did NOT verify**: Global Coding Plan endpoint (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) — not maintained by this contributor.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — removes a suppression; Coding Plan CN users who previously could not select `qwen3.6-plus` can now do so. No existing working configuration is broken.
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: `qwen3.6-plus` may not be available on all Coding Plan CN accounts (account-level activation required).
  - Mitigation: Users without access will receive a vendor-side model-not-found error, same as any unavailable model; no crash or data loss.
- **Risk**: Global Coding Plan (`coding-intl.dashscope.aliyuncs.com`) behavior unknown — suppression removed for that endpoint too as a side effect.
  - Mitigation: If Global Coding Plan does not support `qwen3.6-plus`, affected users will see a vendor error. A follow-up can re-add a suppression scoped to `coding-intl` only if confirmed unavailable there.

target.title: security(ci): mask derived base64 auth header in custom checkout blocks
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(): beta blocker - ` and link the matching `Beta blocker: - ` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation. - Problem: The inline checkout scripts in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` build a Basic-auth header as `base64("x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}")` but never register the derived value with the runner's log scrubber. GitHub Actions only auto-masks the raw `GITHUB_TOKEN`, not values derived from it. - Why it matters: If any downstream step runs under `set -x`, emits verbose `git` tracing, or fails while printing the fetch command, the base64 blob can surface in public CI logs and be trivially decoded back to a live installation token. - What changed: Added `echo "::add-mask::${auth_header}"` immediately after the header is constructed in every custom checkout block (11 jobs), so the runner redacts the value from all subsequent log output. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to job graph, matrices, timeouts, permissions, triggers, runners, or any non-checkout step. Behavior is identical; only the logging surface is hardened. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [x] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: The custom checkout scripts bypass `actions/checkout` and construct their own `AUTHORIZATION: basic ` header. The runner's automatic secret masking only covers the literal `GITHUB_TOKEN` string, so the base64-encoded derivative is unprotected in logs. - Missing detection / guardrail: No lint or workflow audit rule flags derived-secret values that are not passed through `::add-mask::` before use. - Contributing context (if known): Multiple jobs duplicate the same checkout block, so the gap is replicated across every lane that uses it. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [x] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`security-scm-fast` job, zizmor audit step) - Scenario the test should lock in: Any base64-encoded auth header built inline must be passed to `::add-mask::` before the first step that could emit it to logs. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: zizmor already runs on changed workflow files in CI and is the canonical static auditor for this class of issue. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `security-scm-fast` runs zizmor on changed workflows. - If no new test is added, why not: The change is a one-line log-scrubber registration with no runtime behavior to assert; static workflow audit is the correct layer. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes None. ## Diagram (if applicable) For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`. ```text Before: [build auth_header=base64(token)] -> [git fetch ... AUTHORIZATION: basic $auth_header] -> [on verbose/failure: header visible in logs] After: [build auth_header=base64(token)] -> [::add-mask:: auth_header] -> [git fetch ...] -> [runner scrubs value from all logs] ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Risk — a base64-encoded GitHub token derivative could leak via verbose shell traces, git debug output, or error paths that echo the fetch command. Mitigation — register the derived header with the runner's log scrubber via `::add-mask::` in every job that constructs it, so any accidental emission is redacted before reaching the log stream. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (GitHub-hosted / Blacksmith runners per existing `runs-on` matrix) - Runtime/container: default runner image, no container - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): `.github/workflows/ci.yml` ### Steps 1. Push the branch and open this PR so the `CI` workflow runs. 2. Open any job that uses the custom checkout block (e.g. `build-artifacts`, `checks-fast-core`). 3. Inspect the raw log of the `Checkout` step and confirm no base64 `AUTHORIZATION` value is emitted; the existing `zizmor` audit on changed workflows passes. ### Expected - All jobs pass with identical behavior to `main`; any log line that would have contained the base64 header shows `***` instead. ### Actual - Matches expected on the pushed branch. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Demonstration run on my fork showing that `::add-mask::` scrubs a base64 `x-access-token:` header from GitHub Actions logs (dummy value, no real secret involved): - Without mask: `header value is eC1hY2Nlc3MtdG9rZW46ZGVtby12YWx1ZS1ub3QtYS1zZWNyZXQ=` - After `::add-mask::`: `header value is ***` Full run: https://github.com/anurag-bg-neu/openclaw/actions/runs/24701668471/job/72245961669 ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Confirmed the `::add-mask::` line is present in every custom checkout block that constructs `auth_header` (11 occurrences), placed immediately after header construction and before the first `git` invocation. - Edge cases checked: No shell quoting or escaping change; `auth_header` is still expanded the same way in the `http.extraheader` config. Retry loop and checkout semantics unchanged. - What you did **not** verify: Runtime behavior on the Blacksmith self-hosted runner class — indistinguishable from GitHub-hosted since `::add-mask::` is a runner workflow command, not a runner-class feature. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: None beyond the zero-behavior-change surface; `::add-mask::` is a log-scrubber directive and does not alter execution. - Mitigation: Existing `security-scm-fast` zizmor audit continues to gate workflow changes.

target.title: fix(cron): make isolated agent watchdogs configurable
target.body:
## Summary
- raises isolated-agent cron setup/pre-execution watchdog defaults from 60s to 3m
- adds `cron.isolatedAgentSetupWatchdog` and `cron.isolatedAgentPreExecutionWatchdog` config fields
- documents the new knobs and adds schema/timer regression coverage

## Why
Some isolated agent cron jobs can spend more than 60s in provider/runtime startup before first execution progress, especially on loaded hosts or with larger bootstraps. The previous hardcoded watchdog could abort these jobs at the pre-execution dispatch phase even when direct agent execution is healthy.

## Safety
- existing configs continue to work
- setup watchdog remains separate from the execution timeout budget so queued runner-lane time does not spend `timeoutSeconds`
- pre-execution watchdog remains capped to half of the job timeout
- invalid non-positive duration config is rejected by schema validation
- failure diagnostics remain phase-specific

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated `agentTurn` cron jobs could fail during setup or pre-execution startup at the old hardcoded 60s watchdog, even when the underlying agent execution was otherwise healthy.
- **Real environment tested:** Corroborating live proof from @kip-claw on a self-hosted Raspberry Pi 5 (4 GB, ARM64), Debian bookworm, OpenClaw 2026.5.12 (`f066dd2`), systemd service, OAuth via `openai-codex`, `openai/gpt-5.4` primary with `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` fallback. See Kip's PR comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82223#issuecomment-4470372274
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Kip reproduced the failure with 19 isolated cron jobs, patched both built cron watchdog constants from `60000` to `180000`, cleared Node compile cache, restarted Gateway, and reran the affected isolated cron class. This PR matches that observed fix by making both setup and pre-execution watchdog defaults `3m` through typed source/config/docs coverage.
- **Evidence after fix:** Kip's redacted live output shows the representative run progressing beyond the old 60s watchdog and completing:

```text
06:40:38.036  lane dequeue
06:40:50.488  session state -> processing (runner started, 12s)
06:42:48.542  session state -> idle (run_completed)
06:42:48.622  embedded run done: durationMs=130542 aborted=false
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The Pi hardware health check job changed from `error` to `ok` and completed successfully in about 130s with `aborted=false`, which is inside the new 180s default setup/pre-execution watchdog budget.
- **What was not tested:** No production cron cutover, public side effects, or unrelated cron lanes were exercised by this PR proof. The exact PR-head source/config path is covered by schema and timer regression tests; Kip's live proof validates the same 180s runtime behavior in a real OpenClaw deployment.

Additional redacted local Gateway proof from a real OpenClaw setup:

Before the watchdog change, representative isolated agent cron runs failed during startup/pre-execution cleanup with the phase-specific diagnostic below, even though direct agent execution was healthy:

```text
status: error
error: cron: isolated agent run stalled before execution start (last phase: attempt-dispatch)
durationMs: ~60000
```

After applying the equivalent local runtime change to use a longer isolated-agent setup/pre-execution watchdog and restarting the Gateway, the same representative isolated cron class completed instead of being aborted at the old 60s ceiling:

```text
status: ok
error: null
startup/pre-execution time: >60000ms and <300000ms
```

This PR turns that local recovery into typed config, schema validation, docs, and regression coverage rather than keeping a dist-only local patch. The public evidence above is intentionally redacted to avoid exposing local paths, private job names, or private network details.

## Test plan
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [ ] Local targeted Vitest rerun after matching Kip's 180s proof is pending CI or a dependency-restored checkout; this clean local checkout has no `node_modules`, so `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` cannot resolve `vitest/package.json` without installing dependencies.
